cot_prefix: Okay, let's tackle this question. The passage is about saving money during a trip to Disney World. The user wants to know which of the four options are correct based on the text. First, let me read through the passage again to make sure I understand the key points. The main advice given is to plan in advance, choose the right time of year (avoiding peak seasons), stay outside the resort if planning for a week or two, utilize budget hotels with shuttle services, buy food ahead of time, eat at the hotel or cheaper restaurants instead of in the park, and carry your own food and drinks. Looking at the options: A) By going there in the peak seasons. The passage says peak seasons are costly, so this is actually something to avoid. So A is incorrect. B) By staying outside the resort for a week or two. The passage mentions that staying outside the resort can save money if you're staying for a week or two. So B is correct. C) By buying food ahead of time in Disney World. Wait, the passage says to buy food ahead of time, but not in Disney World. It suggests having meals at the hotel or cheaper restaurants, and avoiding buying food in or around the park. So buying ahead of time, but not in Disney World.
